Package org.areslib.math.kinematics

Class KinematicAiming

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.math.kinematics.KinematicAiming
public class KinematicAiming extends Object
Utility for "Shoot-On-The-Move" kinematic aiming.

Calculates the required lead angle to hit a static target while the robot is moving, by factoring in the robot's current field-relative velocity and the projectile's expected muzzle velocity. This allows a robot to confidently hit targets while strafing at high speeds.

  • Method Details

    • calculateAim

      public static KinematicAiming.AimResult calculateAim(ChassisSpeeds robotVelocityFieldRelative, Translation2d robotPosition, Translation2d targetPosition, double projectileSpeedMetersPerSec)
      Calculates the required aiming parameters to hit a static target while moving.
      Parameters:
      robotVelocityFieldRelative - The current velocity of the robot relative to the field. (e.g., from ChassisSpeeds with robot heading).
      robotPosition - The current position of the robot.
      targetPosition - The position of the target.
      projectileSpeedMetersPerSec - The muzzle velocity of the projectile.
      Returns:
      The AimResult containing the required heading and virtual target.
